hero.subtitle: Tradenza is a modern, open-source trading journal. Import your trades, review every execution, and turn your history into an edge — all in one calm, focused place.
hero.note: No credit card — use it free in your browser, or self-host.

features.heading: Everything else you'd expect
features.subheading: Beyond the dashboard, journal and discipline tracker — playbooks, analytics and the tooling that makes daily logging fast and keeps your data yours.

strategies.title: Strategies & playbooks
strategies.desc: Define each setup you trade as a reusable strategy with entry and exit checklists and a reference screenshots. Assign it to trades, tick off what you actually followed, and review per-strategy stats and how closely you stuck to the plan.

import.title: Flexible import
import.desc: A guided wizard imports CSV exports from a broad catalog of brokers — with automatic de-duplication and a full import history.

accounts.title: Trading accounts
accounts.desc: Built for the prop-firm workflow: track firms, phases, account sizes and starting balances, and filter everything by account.

tags.title: Tags & categories
tags.desc: Color-coded tags grouped into categories — label setups, mistakes and conditions, then filter and analyze by them.

responsibility.title: Responsive app
responsibility.desc: Designed to work across desktop, tablet and mobile screens, so you can review trades and check your journal wherever you are.

filters.title: Global filters
filters.desc: One header to switch accounts, pick a date range with presets, toggle $ / R and more — applied across every screen.

showcase.heading: Real screens, real workflow
showcase.subheading: Here's how a trading session comes together.

dashboard.title: See your whole edge at a glance
dashboard.desc: A customizable widget dashboard with the metrics that actually move the needle — net P&L, win rate, profit factor, a trade score, your equity curve and a P&L calendar.
points: Drag-and-drop widgets and saved layout templates
Equity curve, P&L calendar and KPI tiles
Filter by account, date range, $ / R and more

trade.title: Review every trade like a pro
trade.desc: Open any trade on a full price chart with your executions marked, watch the running P&L build, rate your execution and journal the setup, emotions, mistakes and lessons — then arrange the sidebar panels and stats exactly how you like.
points: Chart with entries, exits and running P&L
Structured notes, tags, screenshots, star rating and more
Customizable sidebar — show, hide and reorder every panel and stat

discipline.title: Process over P&L
discipline.desc: Hold yourself to your own rules, separate from P&L. Split non-negotiable limits from quality habits, check them off each day, and watch a year-long heatmap paint the health of your discipline at a glance. Streaks and consistency stats keep you honest — and a payoff view shows whether discipline actually pays.
points: Hard rules vs. soft habits, each on its own daily schedule
Year heatmap, clean-day streaks and a 30-day discipline trend
See if discipline pays: daily P&L and R-multiple by day type

how.heading: From raw trades to a real edge

step1.title: Import or log your trades
step1.desc: Bring in a CSV from your broker, or add trades manually.

step2.title: Review & journal
step2.desc: Open each trade on the chart, grade your execution, and capture the setup, emotions and lessons while they're fresh.

step3.title: Improve with data
step3.desc: Watch your statistics and discipline streaks, find your edge — and your leaks — and trade the plan, not the feeling.

oss.heading: Free, open, and yours to keep
oss.desc: Tradenza is free and open source. Use it however you like, self-host it on your own infrastructure, read every line, and contribute if you'd like. There's no paywall and no lock-in — your trading data is yours.
oss.tipLead: If it helps your trading, a voluntary tip keeps it going — entirely optional.

items.a: Yes. Sign up and use the hosted app for free, and the whole project is open source under AGPL-3.0 so you can self-host it too. No paywalled features and no trial that expires. If it helps your trading, a voluntary tip is welcome — but never required.

items.q: Hosted or self-hosted — what's the difference?
items.a: Two ways to run Tradenza, both free. Use the hosted app right here — sign up and start journaling, with nothing to install or maintain. Or, because it's open source, deploy your own instance and keep everything on your own infrastructure. Same app either way; pick whatever you prefer.

items.q: Do I need to be a developer to use it?
items.a: Not at all. Just sign up and use Tradenza in your browser — there's nothing to install. If you're technical and want full control, the project is open source and self-hostable; the README walks through it step by step.

items.q: Where is my trading data stored?
items.a: On the hosted app your trades live in a secure managed database, tied to your account — you can export or delete them at any time, and your data is never sold. Prefer full control? Self-host and your data never leaves your own infrastructure.

items.q: Which brokers and instruments are supported?
items.a: Import works from a broad catalog of broker and platform CSV exports — Interactive Brokers, MetaTrader 4/5, Tradovate, Thinkorswim, cTrader, DeepCharts and many more — with automatic de-duplication. Stocks, futures, forex, crypto and options are supported, and futures P&L uses built-in contract multipliers so the numbers are correct per instrument.

items.q: Can I contribute or request features?
items.a: Absolutely. It's open source and built in the open — open an issue for bugs or ideas, or send a pull request. See CONTRIBUTING.md to get started.

items.q: Does Tradenza connect to my broker or place trades?
items.a: No. Tradenza is a journaling and analytics tool only. It never connects to live brokerage accounts, never places trades and never moves money — you stay fully in control.

finalCta.heading: Start journaling today
finalCta.subheading: Build the habit that compounds. Your future self will thank you.

footer.disclaimer: Tradenza is a journaling and analytics tool. It does not place trades or provide financial advice. Trading involves risk.
